steps.title: Interpret the exponential growth factor
steps.content:
For every increase of 2 in the input, f(a) multiplies by a constant factor c. This means:
$$f(a+2) = c \cdot f(a)$$
We need to find what c equals, then identify which form shows it explicitly.

steps.title: Calculate c from one of the forms
steps.content:
Use form C since it is simplest: $f(x) = 48(64)^x$

When $x$ increases by 2:
$$f(x+2) = 48(64)^{x+2} = 48(64)^x \cdot (64)^2 = f(x) \cdot 4,096$$

Therefore, c = 4,096. (All four forms are equivalent, so this holds for any choice.)

steps.title: Find which form displays c as the base
steps.content:
Check form D: $f(x) = 48(4,096)^{\frac{x}{2}}$

When $x$ increases by 2:
$$f(x+2) = 48\left(4,096\right)^{\frac{x+2}{2}} = 48\left(4,096\right)^{\frac{x}{2}+1} = 48\left(4,096\right)^{\frac{x}{2}} \cdot 4,096 = f(x) \cdot 4,096$$

In form D, the base 4,096 equals c and is displayed explicitly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
